data.sentence: Hey Nathan! Good to see you on here, how are you doing today?
Doing really well, thanks Aisha. Busy as always, you know how it is in retail right now, but yeah, excited for this call actually.

Ha, I can imagine! I also wanted to introduce you to Sofia Petrov — she's one of our senior engineers on the Comply side of things, and she's going to be really helpful for what we're walking through today.
Hey Nathan, great to meet you. Really looking forward to digging into your SOC 2 prep situation and seeing how we can help.

Yeah, likewise Sofia. And honestly, the timing on this couldn't be better for us. We've got an auditor engagement scoped for, uh, Q2 — so probably May or June — and we're kind of scrambling to get our evidence collection and reporting story together.

Perfect, yeah that's exactly why we wanted to get this on the calendar. And May-June is actually a really workable timeline for what we're going to show you. So, Nathan, maybe just to set the stage — can you give us a quick sense of where Maplewood is right now with SOC 2? Like what's your current process for pulling together audit evidence?

Yeah so, honestly? It's pretty manual right now. We've got a shared drive with a bunch of folders, we're pulling logs from like four different places, and the last time we went through this — it was maybe eighteen months ago — it basically consumed two of my engineers for almost three weeks. Which, you know, is just not sustainable.

Three weeks of engineer time, yeah, that's — that's a really common pain point we hear. And that's actually the exact problem Aegis Comply is built to solve. Sofia, do you want to kind of walk Nathan through how the evidence collection works on our end?

Sure, absolutely. So, at a high level, Comply continuously maps your cloud environment against the SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria — we're talking access controls, availability, change management, incident response, all of that. And rather than your team having to go hunting for evidence when the auditor shows up, the platform is already collecting it in real time and organizing it into a structured evidence library.
Okay, so like — when you say evidence library, is that something I can actually hand to an auditor directly? Or is it more of an internal thing?

Great question. So right now, you can export the evidence packages and share them externally — it's formatted in a way that auditors are used to seeing. And actually, um, we have something pretty exciting coming down the pipeline that's going to make that even more turnkey.

Yeah, I was going to mention that actually. So we're launching Comply v2 — it's not out yet but it's coming very soon — and one of the headline features is on-demand compliance reporting. So instead of exporting a package manually, you can generate a full SOC 2 report on demand, right from the dashboard. It's going to be a big deal.
Wait, so like a full report? As in something I could send to an auditor or a customer who's asking for our compliance posture?

Exactly. And it's not just SOC 2 either — the v2 launch is going to support multiple frameworks. So PCI DSS, HIPAA, ISO 27001 as well. I know you're focused on SOC 2 right now but given that you're in retail, PCI probably isn't far from your mind either.

Oh, PCI is absolutely on our radar. We process a decent volume of card transactions and, uh, yeah, keeping up with that is — it's a lot. So the fact that those could potentially come from the same platform is really interesting.

Right, and that's kind of the vision, right? One platform, continuous compliance across your key frameworks, instead of running separate workstreams for each one. Sofia, can you talk a bit about how the framework mapping works when you're dealing with overlapping controls?

Yeah so this is actually one of my favorite parts of the product. The control library is built so that overlapping requirements across frameworks are mapped to a single underlying control. So if you have, say, an access review process, that single control can satisfy requirements in SOC 2, PCI DSS, and ISO 27001 simultaneously. You're not duplicating your effort — the platform does that work for you.

That is — okay, that is actually really smart. Because right now that is a huge headache. We've got separate checklists for everything and there's so much duplication, it's — yeah, it drives me crazy honestly.

Yeah totally, we hear that all the time. And look, even before v2 drops, there's a lot Comply can do for you right now in terms of getting your environment mapped and your evidence organized ahead of your Q2 audit. So even if the on-demand reporting isn't live yet when you start, you'd be well set up to take advantage of it once it is.

That makes sense. And speaking of getting set up — what does the onboarding actually look like? Like, how long does it typically take before the platform is, you know, actually doing something useful for us?

So it depends a little on your environment complexity, but for a retail org of your size we typically see customers go from initial integration to having a meaningful control coverage picture within two to three weeks. The connectors to your cloud infrastructure are the main lift, and then the framework mapping is largely automated from there.
Two to three weeks, okay. And is that, uh — is that with support from your team, or is it more of a self-serve thing?

Definitely not self-serve, we're very much hands-on during onboarding. You'd have Sofia's team supporting the technical integration, and I'd be your main point of contact for everything on the CS side — making sure timelines are on track, helping you prioritize which controls to tackle first based on your audit scope. We've done this with a few other retail customers and we know the common pitfalls, so we try to help people avoid them.

That's reassuring to hear, yeah. I think my biggest concern honestly is just the time investment from my team's side. Like, I've got three engineers and they're all stretched. So if the integration is — if it's going to be another big lift I need to think about how to resource it.

Totally fair concern. I think what I'd say is — the upfront time investment is real but it's much more contained than what you described for your last audit cycle. And the return on that investment is that your next audit and every audit after that becomes dramatically less painful. Like, we're talking potentially going from three engineer-weeks down to a few days.

Yeah, if that's actually the outcome that would be — that would be massive for us. Honestly, just getting time back for my team to work on actual infrastructure work instead of compliance paperwork is a huge win.

A hundred percent. And that's exactly the feedback we get from customers after they've been through their first audit cycle on Comply. It just — it changes the dynamic. Compliance stops being this fire drill and starts being more of an ongoing process.
Okay so — yeah, I think I'm pretty sold on the concept. Can we talk about what next steps would look like? Like, if we wanted to move forward, what's the process from here?

Yes, absolutely. So what I'd suggest is — first, let's get a technical scoping session on the calendar with Sofia and whoever on your team would be handling the integration side. That's usually where we get into the specifics of your CloudPrime or Azure setup, whatever you're running, and scope out the connector work.
We're primarily CloudPrime, little bit of Azure. Yeah, a scoping call sounds good. I'd probably loop in my lead engineer — her name's Rachel, she handles most of our cloud infra.

Perfect, CloudPrime is great, we have very deep coverage there. And yeah, definitely bring Rachel in — the more technical context we have upfront, the smoother the integration goes. I'd also love to do a short walkthrough of the Comply dashboard with you and Rachel before that scoping call, just so you can see the interface and have a shared mental model going in.
Yeah, that actually makes a lot of sense. She'll have questions I probably won't even think to ask, so.

Ha, exactly! Okay so I'll send over a calendar invite for a thirty-minute demo session — I'm thinking early next week if that works — and then we can line up the scoping call from there. And I'll also send you a summary of everything we talked about today along with some resources on the SOC 2 framework mapping so you have something to share internally if needed.

That would be super helpful, yeah. I've got a meeting with my director next Friday and it'd be great to have some of that material to reference. He's been asking about our compliance posture for a while so this is — yeah, good timing.
Perfect, we'll make sure you have everything you need for that. Nathan, this was a great call — really excited about working with Maplewood on this.
Same, yeah. Really glad we finally got this on the calendar. Looking forward to the demo. Thanks both of you.
Thanks Nathan, talk soon!
